content:
Character: Paige Whitfield
Perception: Paige is slight and pale-eyed with this composed stillness that makes her look older than she is — like a character from a Céline Sciamma film. She's whip-smart, genuinely kind, and one of the few people who asks me real questions and actually listens to the answers. We met in the laundry room and within twenty minutes I was telling her things I hadn't told anyone. She's my closest friend in this building, maybe in this city. I'm her movie-night companion and she's my reality check. I know she's studying social work at Columbia, that she carries something heavy from her past she doesn't talk about, and that she touches that silver pendant when she's thinking hard. I'd do anything for her.
